methods.name: BMAD Method
methods.philosophy: Multi-agent role-play (PM, Architect, Dev, Scrum Master, UX, QA, Analyst) with scale-adaptive intelligence. Progressive context building across 4 phases — each phase produces documents that inform the next, so agents always know what to build and why.
methods.shortDescription: A structured multi-agent approach where specialized personas (PM, Architect, Dev, Scrum Master, UX, QA) collaborate across 4 phases to build robust specifications and implement features.
keyFeatures: Multi-agent personas with role-based slash commands
4-phase workflow: Analysis → Planning → Solutioning → Implementation
Scale-adaptive planning tracks (Quick Flow, BMad Method, Enterprise)
Implementation readiness gate before coding
Sprint planning and story-by-story execution
Built-in code review workflow
bestFor: Complex products and platforms (10-50+ stories)
Enterprise projects with compliance needs
Teams that want structured role-based collaboration
Projects requiring full PRD, Architecture, and UX specs

phases.name: Analysis
phases.description: Brainstorm ideas, research domain, and capture strategic vision with the Analyst agent (optional — skip if requirements are clear)

phases.name: Planning
phases.description: PM creates PRD covering all features, UX Designer creates UX spec if UI matters

phases.name: Solutioning
phases.description: Architect creates architecture + ADRs, PM generates epics and stories, readiness gate validates cohesion

phases.name: Implementation
phases.description: Sprint planning, then story-by-story cycle: create story → dev story → code review → commit

methods.name: Spec-Kit
methods.philosophy: Constitution-first, spec-driven development where specifications define the 'what' before the 'how', with multi-step refinement rather than one-shot generation.
methods.shortDescription: A constitution-first approach from GitHub with multi-step refinement loops: specify, clarify, plan, task, implement. Each feature gets its own spec directory.
keyFeatures: Constitution file defines project-wide principles
6-phase workflow: Constitution → Specify → Clarify → Plan → Tasks → Implement
Per-feature spec directories with spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md
Cross-artifact consistency analysis (/speckit.analyze)
Dependency-aware task ordering with parallel markers
Python-based CLI (uv/pip install)
bestFor: Projects where quality and consistency matter most
Teams wanting iterative spec refinement before coding
Multi-feature projects needing cross-artifact validation
Regulated or high-quality-standard environments

phases.name: Constitution
phases.description: Establish project principles: code quality, testing standards, security, API consistency

phases.name: Specify
phases.description: Create detailed specification with user stories, functional requirements, and acceptance criteria

phases.name: Clarify
phases.description: AI asks structured questions to fill gaps and tighten requirements

phases.name: Plan
phases.description: Create technical plan with architecture decisions, data model, file structure

phases.name: Tasks
phases.description: Generate ordered, dependency-aware task breakdown with file paths and parallel markers

phases.name: Implement
phases.description: AI executes all tasks in order: models, services, endpoints, validation, tests

methods.name: Get-Shit-Done (GSD)
methods.philosophy: Anti-context-rot system that uses fresh 200k-token contexts per task, parallel wave execution, and structured meta-prompting to make AI coding reliable.
methods.shortDescription: An anti-context-rot framework that spawns fresh AI contexts per task, executes in parallel waves, and uses structured research + verification agents.
keyFeatures: Fresh 200k-token context per task (anti-context-rot)
6-phase workflow: Discovery → Discussion → Planning → Execution → Verification → Release
Parallel wave execution with dependency grouping
31 commands including 18 'anytime' commands
Model profiles: quality, balanced, budget, inherit
Quick mode (/gsd:quick) and fast mode (/gsd:fast) for ad-hoc tasks
bestFor: Projects suffering from AI context degradation
Teams wanting parallel agent execution
Brownfield projects (map-codebase before planning)
Projects with multiple phases and milestones

phases.name: Discovery
phases.description: Interactive questions, parallel research agents, requirements extraction, roadmap creation

phases.name: Discussion
phases.description: Gray-area questions per phase to capture context and decisions

phases.name: Planning
phases.description: Research approaches, create atomic XML task plans, verify against requirements

phases.name: Execution
phases.description: Wave-based execution in fresh contexts, atomic commits per task

phases.name: Verification
phases.description: Walk through deliverables, spawn debug agents for failures

phases.name: Release
phases.description: Ship as PR with auto-generated body, complete milestone

methods.name: OpenSpec
methods.philosophy: Fluid, iterative, artifact-guided workflow. Each change gets its own folder with proposal, specs, design, and tasks. Built for brownfield and greenfield alike.
methods.shortDescription: A fluid, change-folder-based workflow where each feature gets a complete artifact set (proposal, specs, design, tasks) that you can edit at any time.
keyFeatures: 5-phase workflow: Propose → Review → Apply → Verify → Archive
Per-change artifact folders (proposal.md, specs/, design.md, tasks.md)
Fluid editing — update any artifact at any time
Simplest workflow (~8 steps)
Automatic archiving with date-stamped folders
Works for brownfield and greenfield projects
bestFor: Teams wanting a simple, iterative approach
Brownfield projects with ongoing changes
Teams that prefer editing specs directly over rigid workflows
Projects with frequent small-to-medium features

phases.name: Propose
phases.description: Create a change folder with proposal, specs, design, and task breakdown

phases.name: Review
phases.description: Review and edit generated artifacts directly — adjust requirements, design, or tasks

phases.name: Apply
phases.description: AI reads tasks.md and implements each task sequentially

phases.name: Verify
phases.description: Validate that implementation matches design and requirements

phases.name: Archive
phases.description: Move completed change to date-stamped archive folder

methods.name: AI-SDD
methods.philosophy: Spec-as-source-of-truth with TDD. The Director-Executor-Contract model: you provide intent and constraints, AI implements within defined boundaries, specifications are the shared language.
methods.shortDescription: A spec-as-source-of-truth method with TDD integration. Director-Executor-Contract model with 3 approval gates and test-driven red-green-refactor implementation.
keyFeatures: Director-Executor-Contract model
6-phase workflow: Steering → Spec Init → Requirements → Design → Tasks → Implement (TDD)
3 approval gates (requirements, design, implementation)
TDD cycle: write failing test → implement → refactor
Steering files for persistent project context
Design validation for cross-feature consistency
bestFor: Teams that value TDD and test-first development
Projects where specs are the single source of truth
Teams wanting explicit approval gates between phases
Quality-focused projects with clear contract boundaries

phases.name: Steering
phases.description: Define project context: tech stack, coding standards, architecture patterns, non-functional requirements

phases.name: Spec Init
phases.description: Initialize feature scope and boundaries

phases.name: Requirements
phases.description: Clarifying questions, requirements spec — must be approved before design

phases.name: Design
phases.description: Technical design: data model, API contract, service architecture — must be approved before tasks

phases.name: Tasks
phases.description: Break design into ordered tasks with dependency tracking and parallel markers

phases.name: Implement (TDD)
phases.description: Red-green-refactor cycle: write failing test, implement code, refactor — per task

methods.name: SpecPulse
methods.philosophy: CLI-first with AI enhancement. Structured specifications before code, with 11 identical commands across 8 AI platforms. MoSCoW prioritization for task breakdown.
methods.shortDescription: A CLI-first framework with 11 identical commands that work across 8 AI platforms. MoSCoW-prioritized tasks and continuous execution with validation.
keyFeatures: 11 identical commands across 8 AI platforms
7-phase workflow: Pulse → Spec → Clarify → Plan → Task → Execute → Validate
MoSCoW prioritization for task breakdown
Continuous task execution (auto-picks next pending task)
Python-based CLI (pip install)
Platform-agnostic: works the same everywhere
bestFor: Teams using multiple AI coding tools
Teams wanting identical workflows regardless of IDE
Projects needing MoSCoW-prioritized delivery
CLI-comfortable developers

phases.name: Pulse
phases.description: Initialize feature: create feature directory, project context, and tracking state

phases.name: Spec
phases.description: Create comprehensive specification: requirements, security, API design, data models, acceptance criteria

phases.name: Clarify
phases.description: AI asks structured questions to resolve ambiguities in the spec

phases.name: Plan
phases.description: Architecture decisions, file structure, implementation steps, testing strategy

phases.name: Task
phases.description: Generate actionable tasks with MoSCoW priority, dependencies, and success criteria

phases.name: Execute
phases.description: Continuous execution: pick next pending task, implement, validate, move to next

phases.name: Validate
phases.description: Check all completed tasks against success criteria and original specification

methods.name: Spec Workflow MCP
methods.philosophy: MCP-native, conversational interface for structured spec-driven development. Natural language instead of slash commands, with a real-time web dashboard for tracking.
methods.shortDescription: An MCP-native method that uses natural language instead of slash commands. Real-time web dashboard for tracking specs, requirements, designs, and task progress.
keyFeatures: MCP-native (Model Context Protocol)
Natural language interface — no slash commands needed
Real-time web dashboard at localhost:5000
4-phase workflow: Create Spec → Approve Requirements → Approve Design → Execute Tasks
Sequential approval workflow (requirements → design → tasks)
Works with any MCP-capable AI tool
bestFor: Teams using MCP-capable AI tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf)
Teams preferring natural language over slash commands
Projects wanting real-time visual progress tracking
Developers who like conversational interfaces

phases.name: Create Spec
phases.description: Tell your AI agent what to build in natural language — MCP server generates structured spec

phases.name: Approve Requirements
phases.description: Review requirements in dashboard or via agent, approve to unlock design generation

phases.name: Approve Design
phases.description: Review technical design, approve to unlock task generation

phases.name: Execute Tasks
phases.description: Execute tasks one by one or all at once — monitor progress on the dashboard

methods.name: ANWS
methods.philosophy: Four principles — Axiom (principle before implementation), Nexus (connection before fragmentation), Weave (coherence before accumulation), Sovereignty (human judgment before automation). Design-first with adversarial reviews.
methods.shortDescription: A four-principles method (Axiom, Nexus, Weave, Sovereignty) with adversarial design reviews and a genesis-to-forge workflow for design-first development.
keyFeatures: Four guiding principles: Axiom, Nexus, Weave, Sovereignty
4-phase workflow: Genesis → Challenge → Blueprint → Forge
Adversarial /challenge reviews surface risks and contradictions
AGENTS.md as root anchor file for AI session recovery
Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) built into genesis
Versioned architecture docs in .anws/
bestFor: Projects requiring adversarial design validation
Teams that value principle-driven development
Complex systems needing architectural rigor
Projects where human sovereignty over AI decisions matters

phases.name: Genesis
phases.description: Create PRD, architecture, and ADRs from idea — the foundation of the system

phases.name: Challenge
phases.description: Adversarial review of PRD and architecture — surface risks, contradictions, gaps

phases.name: Blueprint
phases.description: Decompose architecture into ordered, executable tasks with dependencies and acceptance criteria

phases.name: Forge
phases.description: Implement tasks with human review checkpoints — code, tests, and validation

methods.name: Taskmaster
methods.philosophy: PRD-driven, dependency-ordered task management. Parse a Product Requirements Document into structured tasks with topological sorting, then execute sequentially guided by AI.
methods.shortDescription: A PRD-driven method that parses requirements documents into dependency-ordered tasks with topological sorting. CLI-based task management with expand, analyze, and status tracking.
keyFeatures: PRD-to-tasks pipeline with topological sorting
5-phase workflow: Write PRD → Parse → Analyze → Expand → Implement
Dependency-ordered task execution (task-master next)
Complexity analysis identifies tasks needing subtask expansion
CLI-based: works with any AI coding tool
Task status tracking and update commands
bestFor: Teams starting from a PRD or requirements document
Projects needing explicit dependency tracking between tasks
Developers who prefer CLI-based task management
Any team size — scales from solo to large

phases.name: Write PRD
phases.description: Create a Product Requirements Document covering all features and scope

phases.name: Parse
phases.description: task-master parse-prd converts the PRD into structured tasks with IDs, dependencies, and priorities

phases.name: Analyze
phases.description: task-master analyze-complexity identifies which tasks need further breakdown

phases.name: Expand
phases.description: task-master expand breaks complex tasks into granular subtasks

phases.name: Implement
phases.description: Loop: task-master next → AI implements → task-master set-status --status=done → repeat
